turnaround_reason: Information services name (Web of Science, ProQuest, Derwent, Cortellis) down 84% from $20 IPO highs to $2.59 on slow organic growth and Onex-engineered debt overhang. The Value Creation Plan launched early 2025 is showing traction: Q1 2026 Academia & Government ACV growth >2%, ProQuest adoption strong, IP segment improving to flat. Potential Life Sciences & Healthcare divestiture would deliver the balance sheet and refocus.
bullish_points: FCF yield is extraordinary: $473M FCF on $1.66B mkt cap = 28% FCF yield — extreme value if real
Q1 2026 EPS beat by 25%, second beat in a row — Value Creation Plan executing
Potential L&H divestiture is a hard catalyst that would dramatically delever ($4.3B debt could fall to manageable levels)
Academic AI products (30-60% time savings) position Clarivate on the RIGHT side of AI vs Gartner's wrong side — librarians WANT these tools
Forward P/E of 3.3 is anomalously cheap; price-to-book 0.35 — multiple value heuristics screaming
bearish_points: Revenue still slightly declining (-1.4% TTM) — the recovery is operational not demand-driven
Debt $4.3B vs $232M cash is the central problem; any acquirer of L&H will haircut the price
Onex (PE sponsor) overhang on the cap table — large stock supply potential if they decide to exit
IP segment still negative growth (-1.3%) — Derwent/CompuMark face same AI commoditization risk as Gartner
Information services is a slow-growth category structurally; even successful turnaround caps upside vs growth names
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings July 29 — potential L&H divestiture progress, ACV growth trajectory across all three segments
catalyst_timing: July 29, 2026 (~2 months); L&H sale could be any time in 2026-2027
suggested_trade: Spot position — best risk/reward in today's batch. The combination of 28% FCF yield, executing turnaround plan, and a real divestiture catalyst is rare. Consider Jan 2027 $3 calls as a leveraged play if you want options exposure (cheap given low IV history). Sizing: 1.5-2x normal position.
key_risk: L&H sale process drags or fails; debt refinancing window in 2027-2028 forces dilutive equity raise; Onex dumps stock.
would_skip_if: Q2 ACV growth turns negative across all three segments; L&H sale process formally cancelled; major debt covenant amendment
